b.label: Self/coupling Hessian split H = H_c + H_s
b.standard_term: diagonal-plus-hollow (diagonal / zero-diagonal) matrix decomposition of the Hessian

evidence.label: Floor 1 — Mean Curvature Decomposition
evidence.summary:
The mean curvature H of an implicit constraint surface decomposes exactly as:

    H = H_c + H_s     (no interaction term)

where:

    H_c = −g^T H_c g / (2|g|³)
    H_s = (|g|² tr(H_s) − g^T H_s g) / (2|g|³)

evidence.label: Theorem 5 — Bilinear Coupling Graph Correspondence (n = 3)
evidence.summary: For constraint surfaces where H_s = 0 everywhere (purely bilinear coupling): (a) K_G = κ_c everywhere on the surface. (b) κ_c = 0 at generic points iff the coupling polynomial factors. (c) Factorability is equivalent to
